segments.text: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said,
I'm forced to reply because I'm really upset!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: What I hear you say insults me, but I know how to reply to you!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Don't you know that since ancient times, since human beings were put on this earth,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: that the triumph of the wicked doesn't last long, that those who reject God are only happy for a short time?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even though they are so tall they reach the heavens, even though their heads reach the clouds,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they will vanish forever like their own excrement. People who knew them will ask, ‘Where have they gone?’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They will vanish like a dream, never to be found, fleeing like a vision of the night.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Those who once saw them will not see them anymore; their families will never set eyes on them again.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Their children will have to pay back the poor; they will have to return their wealth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Though the wicked have bodies that are young and strong, they will die and be buried.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Though evil tastes sweet in their mouths and they hide it under their tongues,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: not letting it go but keeping it in their mouths,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: in their stomachs it turns bitter, becoming like snake venom inside them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They swallow wealth and vomit it up again; God forces it from their stomachs.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They suck in snake venom; the bite of the viper will kill them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They will not live to enjoy the streams, the rivers of milk and honey.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They will have to give back what they have gained and will not have any benefit;  they will not enjoy any of their profits.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For they have oppressed and abandoned the poor; they have seized houses they did not build.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Because their greed was never satisfied,  nothing they liked is left that they didn't consume.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Nothing escapes their ravenous appetites, so their happiness doesn't last long.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even when the wicked have all that they want they run into trouble; all kinds of misery will fall upon them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: While they are busy filling their bellies, God's hostility will burn against them, raining down on them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As they flee to escape an iron weapon, a bronze arrow will strike them down.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The arrow is pulled out of their gall bladder, glistening with blood. They are absolutely terrified!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: All they value will disappear into the darkness; divine fire will destroy them; all they have left will go up in smoke.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The heavens reveal what they have done wrong; the earth will rise up against them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: All their possessions will be carried from their homes; they will be dragged off on the day of God's judgment.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: This is the share the wicked receive from God, the inheritance God says they should have.
segments.speaker: Unknown
